title: Document history dropdown with version selection
purpose: Provides a dropdown interface to view and select historical versions of a document, showing relative timestamps and loading states.
module_context: Exports a React functional component named HistoryDropdown that accepts document ID, current content, and version selection handler as props. Uses a custom hook to fetch document history and version content.

steps.content: Parse the input timestamp into a date object.
Compare the date to today and yesterday to determine relative time formatting.
Format the time portion using 12-hour clock with hours, minutes, and seconds.
IF the date is today, return just the formatted time.
ELSE IF the date is yesterday, prepend 'Yesterday' to the formatted time.
ELSE IF the date is within the last 7 days, prepend the day name to the formatted time.
ELSE format the date with month, day, hours, and minutes.
Initialize state for dropdown open status and loading timestamp.
Create a ref for the dropdown element to handle click outside detection.
Fetch document history and version loading function using a custom hook.
Determine if history is available by checking for changes.
Set up event listener for click outside to close the dropdown.
Handle item click by loading version content and notifying parent of selection.
Render the dropdown button with appropriate disabled state and title.
IF dropdown is open and history exists, render the version list in reverse chronological order.
Check if the click target is outside the dropdown element.
IF the click is outside, close the dropdown.
Set the loading timestamp to indicate content is being fetched.
Load the version content for the selected timestamp.
Clear the loading timestamp after content is fetched.
IF content is successfully loaded, notify parent component of version selection.
Close the dropdown after version selection.
